title: Dryopteris ichtiosma

I can find information on the classification of Dryopteris ichtiosma. 
Dryopteris ichtiosma is classified within the kingdom Plantae, division Pteridophyta, class Polypodiopsida, order Polypodiales, and family Dryopteridaceae. This plant is a species of fern in the genus Dryopteris. It is native to China and is also known by other names such as the Chinese scaly male fern or Dryopteris ichtiosma.

intro: Dryopteris ichtiosma is a type of fern, specifically a member of the genus Dryopteris, which is a group of vascular plants characterized by their leafy fronds and ability to thrive in shaded environments. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, providing relief from inflammation and pain, as well as exhibiting antimicrobial and antioxidant activities, which contribute to its health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Dryopteris ichtiosma include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Dryopteris ichtiosma is often used to make tea, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and ointments to alleviate skin irritations and wounds.
